_meta.note: Road-trip conversation game · casual second person · curly apostrophes only · glossary locked

meta_001: 100 easy conversation questions to trade on a road trip, in a café, or in the car — for each one, you both answer.
html_001: The long drive, side by side
html_003: One phone between you, taking turns asking —
html_007: 100 random questions to pass back and forth — on a road trip, in a café, in the car.
html_008: Easy even if you’ve just met.
html_009: Don’t let just one person answer — for each question,
html_015: Tap the screen and the next envelope arrives
html_016: Tap here! Open the first question ✉️
html_019: 🧺 Picnic for Two — see the other games →
js_001: If you could invite anyone in the world to dinner, who would it be?
js_002: Would you like to be famous? If so, in what way?
js_003: Before making a phone call, do you ever rehearse what you’ll say? Why?
js_004: What would a “perfect” day look like for you?
js_005: When did you last sing to yourself? And to someone else?
js_006: If you could live to 90 with either the mind or the body of a 30-year-old for the last 60 years, which would you choose?
js_007: Do you have a hunch about how you’ll die?
js_008: Find three things the two of you have in common.
js_009: What in your life are you most grateful for?
js_010: If you could change one thing about how you were raised, what would it be?
js_011: Tell your life story in a few minutes — just the parts that matter.
js_012: If you could wake up tomorrow with one new ability, what would you pick?
js_013: If a magic ball could answer anything, what one thing would you ask it?
js_014: Is there something you’ve wanted to do for ages? Why haven’t you yet?
js_015: What’s the most rewarding thing you’ve ever done?
js_016: What do you think a real friendship needs most?
js_017: Tell me about your most treasured memory.
js_019: If you were told today you had a year to live, what would you change about how you live now?
js_020: What does true friendship mean to you?
js_021: How big a part do love and affection play in your life?
js_022: Take turns naming something you admire about each other — up to five each.
js_023: Was your family close? Was your childhood happier, or unhappier, than most?
js_024: How’s your relationship with your mother?
js_025: Say three sentences each that start with “We both…” and feel true.
js_026: Finish this sentence: “I wish I had someone I could share my ______ with.”
js_027: If we grew really close, what’s one thing I’d need to know about you?
js_028: Tell me what you like about me — more honestly than you ever could with a stranger.
js_030: When did you last cry in front of someone? And alone?
js_031: Tell me one thing you already like about me.
js_032: Is there a topic too serious to ever joke about?
js_033: If you died tonight with no chance to reach anyone, whose unsaid words would you regret most? Why haven’t you said them?
js_034: Your house is on fire. Everyone and every pet is safe — you can grab one object. What is it?
js_035: Whose death in your family would hit you the hardest? Why?
js_036: Share a personal worry and ask for advice. Then ask the other person to reflect back how you seem to feel about it.
js_037: If you could teleport right now, where to?
js_038: Three things to take to a desert island — go.
js_039: Best movie or show you’ve seen lately?
js_040: If you were reborn as something other than a person, what?
js_041: One food for the rest of your life — what is it?
js_042: First thing you’d do if you won the lottery?
js_043: What did you want to be as a kid? Far from where you are now?
js_044: One app you could never delete?
js_045: When did you last laugh really hard?
js_048: If $1,000 landed in your account right now, where would it go?
js_053: Do you pack early, or right before you leave?
js_054: What’s your profile photo right now? Why that one?
js_056: When did you last cry? (crying at a show counts)
js_058: What’s your MBTI? Does it fit?
js_059: Which country do you most want to visit?
js_060: Invisible for a day — what would you do?
js_062: Which animal is most like you?
js_063: How do you blow off stress?
js_066: One artist to listen to forever — who?
js_067: Any secret trick of yours for the perfect instant noodles?
js_068: Most ridiculous lie anyone’s told you?
js_072: If you got famous, what for?
js_073: First thing you’d grab in a fire? (people and pets aside)
js_074: Who’s your oldest friend? How’d you meet?
js_075: What are you totally into these days?
js_076: A whole month off — what would you do?
js_077: Good with directions? Worst time you got lost?
js_078: A board game or party game you love?
js_080: Live a day as someone else — who?
js_081: Can you cook? What’s your signature dish?
js_082: What kind of coffee or drink do you like?
js_083: Do you have a favorite word?
js_084: What do you notice first in a person?
js_085: Best trip of your life — where?
js_086: What scared you most as a kid?
js_087: If you could call anyone right now, who?
js_089: If you were a color, which one?
js_090: What do you most feel like doing on a rainy day?
js_091: One foreign language mastered instantly — which?
js_093: The bravest thing you’ve ever done?
js_094: If you opened a restaurant, what would you serve?
js_096: What song instantly lifts your mood?
js_097: A hobby that makes time vanish?
js_098: Last time you thanked someone — for what?
js_099: What kind of driver are you?
js_100: Favorite dessert or kind of café?
js_101: Something you only came to love as an adult?
js_102: Favorite cartoon or game as a kid?
js_103: One book that marked your life?
js_105: Something you really want but it’s too pricey?
js_106: Do you prefer time alone, or time together?
js_107: What do you think is your biggest strength?
js_108: If you got a pet, what would it be?
js_111: A city you’d love to live in someday?
js_112: A childhood dream job that still sounds cool today?
js_113: The last new thing you tried?
js_114: A hidden food spot only you know?
js_115: What’s your role in your friend group?
js_117: One thing you want most right now?
js_118: What draws you to someone? (not personality — the little things)
js_120: Do you like working out? What kind?
js_121: One of your most hilarious cringe moments — go!
js_122: A seasonal smell you love? (like fallen autumn leaves)
js_123: If you wrote a book, what would it be about?
js_124: The last comforting words someone gave you?
js_125: Anything you always listen to on your commute?
js_128: A little secret you haven’t told even close people?
js_129: The most stunning view you’ve ever seen — where?
js_130: Where and what do you think you’ll be doing in 5 years?
js_131: How do you show someone you like them?
js_132: When were you proudest of yourself?
js_133: Right now, who are you most grateful for?
js_134: What kind of adult do you want to be? (or already are?)
js_135: Best moment of your day today?
js_136: What are you looking forward to most on this trip?
js_141: 💗 Set III — as close as it gets
js_148: Well done. Hold on to this feeling.

jst_005: prepare the first question behind the envelope

jst_009: the envelope fades and the letter
jst_011: a new envelope slides up from below
jst_013: already-seen question, so reopen it right away
jst_016: minutes of holding each other’s gaze in silence.
jst_017: Many people cry at this moment. Ready? Begin!
jst_018: Press the button and the 4 minutes begin
jst_023: If the conversation never stopped, you won.
jst_024: Shuffle once more for a fresh order?

_app.title: 100 Questions for Two
_app.desc: Road-trip conversation questions — one phone between you, you both answer.
